title: About Exploris
intro: Exploris is an interactive geography learning app with a 3D globe. Learn countries, capitals and much more in a visual and fun way.
section.countries.title: Countries & data
section.countries.body:
Exploris includes all 195 countries recognised by the United Nations, the most widely accepted international standard. You can also explore unrecognised or disputed territories on the globe.

The globe shows Earth as seen from space. This lets you appreciate the true size of countries — something flat maps can't do.
section.learn.title: How to learn
section.learn.tips.explore: Start in Explore: spin the globe, tap countries to see their info card and get familiar with their shape and location. The Table view helps you associate each country with its capital. Tap any country or capital in the table to see it on the globe.
section.learn.tips.play: When you're ready, jump into Play. Adventure mode starts easy and ramps up as you get answers right. How far can you go?
section.learn.tips.passport: Your ultimate goal is to fill your Passport with stamps. To earn a stamp you must complete a test with zero mistakes.
section.gameTypes.title: Game types
section.gameTypes.intro: There are 6 game types, from easiest to hardest:
section.gameTypes.E: ◯? Identify the country — A country is highlighted on the globe and you pick its name from 4 options.
section.gameTypes.C: ◯→◎ Country to capital — You're given a country and pick its capital from 4 options.
section.gameTypes.D: ◎→◯ Capital to country — You're given a capital and pick which country it belongs to.
section.gameTypes.F: ◎? Identify the capital — A capital is highlighted on the globe and you pick its name.
section.gameTypes.A: ◯ Point to the country — You're given a name and tap the country on the globe.
section.gameTypes.B: ◎ Point to the capital — You're given a capital and tap its location on the globe.
section.gameTypes.adventure: Adventure mode combines all types and adapts to your progress. It's the recommended way to play.
section.gameTypes.stamps: "Point to the country" and "Point to the capital" are the types used in the Passport stamp tests.
section.stats.title: Statistics
section.stats.body:
The Statistics view has two tabs:

• Play — Shows your mastery of each game type. A green ✓ means you got it right; a grey ✓ means inferred mastery (for example, if you can point to a country on the map, you can also identify it).

• Stamp tests — Shows your results in the Passport tests. Tracked independently from Play.

You can switch between mastery indicators (✓/✗) and accuracy percentages. Tap a country to go straight to its card in Explore.
section.sources.title: Data sources
section.sources.body: Data comes from public sources such as the United Nations, Natural Earth and Wikidata.
